component_identification.content: Power Grid includes a Germany and USA map board, 132 houses, 84 resource tokens in four fuel types, money, 54 cards including power plants and Step 3 card, and markers for auction and game steps. Setup chooses connected map areas by player count, seeds starting resource market quantities and prices, prepares initial power plant market, and assigns initial player order. Each player starts with houses, cash, and score marker at zero connected cities.

core_game_loop.content: Each round has five phases: player order, power plant auction, resource buying, building houses, and bureaucracy. Players buy plants, fuel them, expand city networks, and earn income from powered cities. Turn order shifts based on network strength and matters differently by phase. Step transitions change city occupancy and market dynamics.

detailed_rules.content: In auction phase plants are bought via open bidding and market replacement. Resource purchase follows reverse order pressure and strict storage limits per plant. Building requires paying connection cost plus city entry cost while respecting current step occupancy limits. Bureaucracy consumes resources to power cities and pays income from payment table by number of powered cities, then refills resources by chart and updates plant market. Step 2 triggers when threshold connected cities are reached; Step 3 enters via Step 3 card handling and changes market and tempo. Connected cities are not the same as powered cities for income and final victory. Common mistakes include overbuying fuel, ignoring reverse-order advantages, and triggering step changes before your plant portfolio is ready.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Game end triggers when a player reaches the required connected city threshold for the player count. Final winner is the player who can power the most cities in the final bureaucracy. Ties break by money then connected cities. During play, score track mainly tracks connection progress and player order position.

beginner_strategy.content: Protect cash in early rounds; do not overspend in auctions. Buy plants with a clear upgrade path and fuel plan. Anticipate resource price spikes and pre-buy efficiently. Expand where future connection options remain open. Time step transitions to your advantage instead of blindly racing the city threshold.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint with rulebook-text as primary source.
